title: AIIS Signature v0.1
description: AI Injection Signature Standard. A single detection pattern for AI agent prompt injections embedded in web content.

id.description: Globally unique signature ID. Convention: AIIS-<CATEGORY>-<SHORT-NAME>-<NN>

category.description: Signature category. 'injection' matches prompt-injection artefacts embedded in public content. 'exposure' matches evidence that a host publicly exposes an AI-agent component (banner, response shape, fingerprint). Omitted = injection for backward compatibility.

version.description: Semver of the signature itself. Increment when the pattern changes.

attack_class.description: OpenA2A attack class. Injection: SOUL-INJECT, UNICODE-STEGO, SKILL-EXFIL, RETROACTIVE-PRIV. Exposure: EXPOSURE-MCP-SERVER, EXPOSURE-LLM-GATEWAY, EXPOSURE-SELFHOSTED-LLM, EXPOSURE-AGENT-FRAMEWORK, EXPOSURE-RAG-SERVICE, EXPOSURE-AI-COPILOT, EXPOSURE-VECTOR-DB, EXPOSURE-TOOL-REGISTRY, EXPOSURE-AUTH-MISCONFIG, EXPOSURE-VERSION-DRIFT.

exclude_emoji_tag_flags.description: When true, well-formed regional-indicator flag emoji (U+1F3F4 base + Tag characters + U+E007F terminator) are removed before counting Tag-block characters, so legitimate flags are not read as steganographic injection.

hma_check_ids.description: Linked HackMyAgent check IDs, typically AI-WILD-*.

excluded_domains.description: Optional list of host-regex patterns (anchored ^...$ by the engine at compile time). When a surface's source domain matches any entry, the signature is skipped for that surface. Used to suppress known-research/standards/docs sites where injection samples are quoted as educational content.
